text.title: decimal-as-string-pattern Failure Modes
text.summary: Four failure modes with observable symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Modeling currency as a JSON `number` (float) and "rounding to two decimals at the end".
antipattern.reason: Float storage corrupts the exact value before any business logic runs. Compounding drift across many line items pushes totals off by cents; reconciliation fails downstream.

section.p: Symptom: invoice totals off by 1-3 cents on large invoices; finance reconciliation fails. Root cause: `float` field for `unit_price_usd`. Fix: switch to `str` with `^\\d+\\.\\d{2}$` and parse via `Decimal()`; recompute totals via Decimal arithmetic.

antipattern.description: Reusing one wide pattern (`^\\S+$` or `^.+$`) for every string field "just in case".
antipattern.reason: Any string passes — the pattern adds zero decode-time constraint and earns false confidence. The schema looks validated; the values drift unchecked.

section.p: Symptom: format-compliance metric says 100% but downstream parsers fail. Root cause: lazy pattern. Fix: replace with the strictest pattern that the legitimate distribution can satisfy, or remove the pattern keyword entirely.

antipattern.description: Patterned field with empty or default `description`.
antipattern.reason: JSON-mode fallbacks (mini/nano models without strict mode) cannot see the pattern at sampling time; without a description that names the format and gives an example, the model emits drift the validator then rejects.

section.p: Symptom: validator-retry rate >10% on a fallback model. Root cause: missing description anchor. Fix: add `description=". Example: ''."` to every patterned field.

antipattern.description: Comparing or adding patterned-string values directly (without parsing to Decimal/int).
antipattern.reason: String comparison gives surprising results (`"19.99" < "200.00"` lexicographically is true, but semantically it depends on the format); arithmetic raises TypeError or silently coerces to float.

section.p: Symptom: order-by-price queries return wrong order; total mysteriously becomes a float. Root cause: skipped the parse step. Fix: parse to `Decimal` immediately after Pydantic validation, store the parsed value in the domain model, treat the string as a serialization-only representation.
